bias_defense_notes: Post-shuffle constraint check: position counts — pos0=1, pos1=2, pos2=1, pos3=1. max_share=2/5=0.40, which satisfies the <=0.40 threshold. All 4 positions represented (quiz has <6 MCQs so the all-4-positions rule is advisory, but the distribution covers all four regardless).
length_bias_revision_notes: Critic found correct answer was longest option in 4/5 questions (80%, threshold 40%). Distractors for q1, q2, q3, q4 expanded to be comparable in length to the correct answer. Correct-answer positions unchanged. q5 was within threshold and not modified.

questions.objective_ref: Define what a foundation model is and explain how it differs from the narrow, task-specific AI systems that came before it.
Explain what 'trained once at scale' means and why the scale of training matters.
Describe what 'usable for many tasks' means, and give two examples of different tasks the same foundation model can perform.
Identify at least three real-world foundation models by name and describe the type of input each one handles.
Explain the general idea of adaptation — that a foundation model can be adjusted or guided for a specific use — without confusing adaptation with the original training process.
